description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a dark bluish-gray coloration and prominent white veining running across its surface. Scattered patches of bright blue luminescence are visible, giving the stone a unique and somewhat otherworldly appearance. The surface is mostly rough and matte, with some areas appearing slightly smoother due to natural wear. One side features three distinct, parallel scratch marks that stand out in a lighter blue shade. The stone's form is angular, with several flat and sloped faces, and it appears dense and solid. Its overall look suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative feature in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A large, dark bluish-gray stone with white veining, blue luminescent patches, and rough, angular surfaces. Notable features include parallel scratch marks and a dense, solid appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting the intricate white veining and subtle blue patches.
description_view_1: This side reveals three prominent, parallel blue scratch marks and more concentrated blue luminescent spots.
description_view_2: Here, the stone's angular edge and a large blue patch on the lower side are visible, with the scratch marks partially in view.
description_view_3: This angle displays another broad face with a large, bright blue area near the base and less visible veining.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Prominent white veining with irregular, natural patterns and scattered blue luminescent patches; one side has parallel scratch marks.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte, with some smoother areas indicating moderate natural weathering but no heavy erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by grass or mulch.
